topic: LLM Function Calling and Tool Use Guide 2026: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google

competitors.title: LLM Function Calling: The Complete Multi-Provider Guide [2026]
key_points: Comprehensive guide covering OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google Gemini function calling
Same get_weather tool implemented across all three providers with side-by-side Python code
5-step universal pattern: define tools, send request with tools, detect tool calls, execute functions, return results
Key format differences: OpenAI uses 'tools' array with 'function' type, Anthropic uses 'input_schema' and 'tool_use' blocks, Google uses 'FunctionDeclaration' objects
Parallel tool calls supported by all providers for performance optimization
Security threats include prompt injection via tool arguments and confused deputy attacks (OWASP LLM06: Excessive Agency)
Decision framework for function calling vs structured outputs vs MCP (Model Context Protocol)
Production best practices: validate arguments, scope tool permissions, require human approval for destructive operations

competitors.title: Function Calling & Tool Use: The Complete Guide for GPT, Claude, and Gemini (2026)
key_points: Complete implementation with working Python code for each provider, tested against 2026 APIs
Detailed comparison tables showing format differences across providers
Parallel tool calls optimization: 3.7x latency speedup from LLMCompiler paper (ICML 2024)
Multi-step agent loops with safeguards: timeout handling, loop detection, error recovery
Error handling patterns for malformed arguments, hallucinated function names, timeouts, and unexpected results
Production best practices: descriptive tool definitions, tool count minimization, output sanitization, comprehensive logging
Type-safe function definitions with Pydantic for validation
Cost tracking per agent execution (tool definitions add 100-300 tokens per tool)

stats.fact: Parallel tool calls provide up to 3.7x latency speedup compared to sequential execution
Function calling is OWASP LLM06: Excessive Agency vulnerability - the #1 LLM security threat
Tool definitions add 100-300 input tokens per tool, increasing API costs
OpenAI introduced function calling in June 2023, now evolved to 'tools' parameter format
Anthropic offers built-in server-side tools: web_search, code_execution, text_editor - unique feature
Gemini 2.5+ supports streaming function call arguments for latency-sensitive applications
Prompt injection via tool arguments is the primary attack vector for function calling systems
OpenAI's strict: true structured outputs mode is incompatible with parallel function calls

angles: Provider format differences challenge: comparing OpenAI's 'tools' array vs Anthropic's 'input_schema' vs Google's 'FunctionDeclaration'
Security-first approach: implementing validation, authorization checks, and audit logging for production function calling
Performance optimization: leveraging parallel tool calls and streaming arguments for latency-sensitive applications
Multi-provider agent framework: using abstraction layers (LiteLLM, Ofox.ai) to write code once, deploy across providers
Production readiness: error handling, cost tracking, and monitoring for large-scale function calling deployments
Future trends: MCP standardization, server-side tools, and streaming argument evolution in 2026-2027
Decision framework: when to use function calling vs structured outputs vs MCP based on use case requirements
